meeting: Post Quantum Transaction Signature (PQTS) Breakout #6 - January 2025

protocol_developments.highlight: Frame transaction/native AA no longer headliner for Hegotá fork
NIST released limited-use SLHDSA parameters: 2^24 signatures, ~3-4KB size

implementation_updates.highlight: Daisugi testnet live: Erigon node with Falcon/Dilithium/ephemeral ECDSA support
NTT/LEGO approach requires scheme-specific precompiles; abstraction doesn't work well
Direct precompiles much lower gas than NTT, especially Dilithium (~100k difference)

hardware_constraints.highlight: NIST SLHDSA-24: ~6 minutes signing time on Ledger hardware wallet
New NIST parameters still impractical: millions of hashes needed per signature
Custom Keccak-based parameters achieve 100-1000x faster signing than NIST proposal

strategy_discussion.highlight: Proposed dual approach: ephemeral keys for low-value, Dilithium for high-value transactions
Ephemeral key security concerns: censorship attacks and long mempool times
Formal verification critical for Solidity-based PQ signature contracts

action_items.action: Publish EVM-friendly SLHDSA parameter research with Keccak instantiation
Send NIST feedback at tomorrow's workshop on hash function crypto-agility
Present full Dilithium ledger integration round-trip demo

decisions.decision: Lesson learned: NTT/LEGO abstraction approach doesn't work for PQ signatures
Solidity contracts for PQ verification must be formally verified, not precompiles

targets.target: Two weeks - Next PQTS breakout with Matteo's ephemeral key updates
